Let $X = \begin{pmatrix}
            7 &  8 &  9 \\
            8 & -9 & -7 \\
           -7 & -7 &  9
         \end{pmatrix}$,
    $Y = \begin{pmatrix}
            9 &  8 & -9 \\
            8 & -7 &  7 \\
            7 &  9 &  8
         \end{pmatrix}$,
let $A = Y^{-1} - X$ and let $B$ be the inverse of $X + A^{-1}$. Find a matrix
$M$ such that $M^2 = XY - BY$ (you may assume that $A$ and $X^{-1} + A^{-1}$
are invertible).
